message.reasoning_content: Speculative multi-token prediction improves local decoding throughput by using a smaller, faster "draft" model to generate several candidate tokens in parallel, then verifying them in a single pass with the larger "target" model—effectively amortizing the computational cost of autoregressive decoding and enabling batch verification of multiple tokens per forward pass. However, this approach may not help when the draft model's predictions are poor (low acceptance rate), causing frequent rejections that negate the speedup, or in contexts where the bottleneck shifts from compute-bound generation to memory-bound token sampling, or when the overhead of managing multiple token streams outweighs the gains from parallel verification.
